title: Fix member login after membership reactivation

requirements.summary: Clear cached authentication state and fetch fresh membership status on each member login attempt to properly handle reactivated memberships
acceptanceCriteria: The frontend clears any cached authentication state when a member attempts to log in
Login errors for reactivated members are resolved and do not show the 'contact gym' message inappropriately
The authentication hook fetches fresh membership status on each login attempt

file_operations.description: Modify the member login flow to clear any cached membership data before validating credentials. Add logic to invalidate or refetch member profile queries immediately after successful login validation to ensure the latest membership status is retrieved from the backend. Remove any stale status checks that might prevent reactivated members from logging in.

Update the login submission handler to explicitly clear query cache for member-related data before calling the login function. Ensure error messages distinguish between invalid credentials and actual inactive status. Add a query invalidation call after successful login to force fresh data fetch.

requirements.summary: Ensure membership status updates in admin MembersTab immediately persist and invalidate cached member queries
acceptanceCriteria: When admin changes a member status from paused to active, the change is immediately reflected in the backend
Query invalidation ensures the updated status is visible across all admin views
No stale cached data prevents the status change from taking effect

file_operations.description: Review and ensure the member update mutation includes comprehensive query invalidation for all member-related queries after status changes. Add specific invalidation for member profile, membership validation, and attendance queries to prevent stale cached data. Verify the mutation success handler properly awaits invalidation completion before showing success feedback.

Review the useUpdateMember mutation hook to ensure it invalidates all relevant query keys including member profiles, member lists, and validation queries. Add explicit invalidation for validateMemberLogin query key if it exists in the cache. Ensure the mutation properly handles optimistic updates or disables cached queries during status transitions.
